prompt: Which courses have been offered in the most sections overall?

Use LEFT JOIN to include courses with zero sections.","sql":"SELECT c.id AS course_id, c.code AS course_code, c.name AS course_name, COUNT(cs.id) AS sections_offered
FROM public.courses AS c
LEFT JOIN public.course_sections AS cs ON cs.course_id = c.id
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
ORDER BY sections_offered DESC, c.code ASC, c.name ASC;","assumptions":["courses.id is the primary key and course_sections.course_id references courses.id (confirmed in schema)","We count every row in course_sections as one offered section (across all semesters/years)","Include courses with zero sections (LEFT JOIN); ties are deterministically ordered by course_code then course_name"]},"endedAt":1771804476964},"run-raw-sql":{"payload":{"sql":"SELECT c.id AS course_id, c.code AS course_code, c.name AS course_name, COUNT(cs.id) AS sections_offered

visualization.title: Courses offered in the most sections (ranked)

resultRaw.sql:
SELECT c.id AS course_id, c.code AS course_code, c.name AS course_name, COUNT(cs.id) AS sections_offered
FROM public.courses AS c
LEFT JOIN public.course_sections AS cs ON cs.course_id = c.id
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
ORDER BY sections_offered DESC, c.code ASC, c.name ASC;

visualization.title: Courses offered in the most sections (ranked)

result.sql:
SELECT c.id AS course_id, c.code AS course_code, c.name AS course_name, COUNT(cs.id) AS sections_offered
FROM public.courses AS c
LEFT JOIN public.course_sections AS cs ON cs.course_id = c.id
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
ORDER BY sections_offered DESC, c.code ASC, c.name ASC;
